zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Margaret Lucombe of Stevenstone於1405年出生在英國肯特易亞斯特維爾時，她的父親John Lucombe III21歲，母親Lady Margaret Cornwall Kayle20歲。她於1429年與Sir Walter Moyle在英國結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1493年6月1日在她的家鄉享年88歲。

nameMeanings.name: Lucombe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Devon): habitational name from any of the five villages of this name in Devon or from Loscombe in Powerstock, Dorset, all probably named from Old English hlōse ‘pigsty’ + cumb ‘valley’ (see Coombe ).

variants.name: Coombe
meanings: topographic name from Middle English combe (Old English cumb, of Celtic origin) denoting a bowl-shaped valley, or else a habitational name from a place so named. A large number of places in southwestern England, mostly spelled Combe, are named with this word.
nickname from Middle English come (Old English cuma) ‘guest, stranger, newcomer’. Compare Coombs .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margaret Lucombe of Stevenstone was born in 1405, in Eastwell, Kent, England, her father, John Lucombe III, was 21 and her mother, Lady Margaret Cornwall Kayle, was 20. She married Sir Walter Moyle in 1429, in England. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 1 June 1493, in her hometown, at the age of 88.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Margaret Lucombe of Stevenstone est née en 1405, à Eastwell, Kent, Angleterre, son père, John Lucombe III, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Lady Margaret Cornwall Kayle, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Walter Moyle en 1429, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 juin 1493, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 88 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Lucombe of Stevenstoneが1405年にイギリスケントエアストウエルで生まれた時，父John Lucombe IIIは21歳，母Lady Margaret Cornwall Kayleは20歳であった。1429年にイギリスでSir Walter Moyleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1493年6月1日に故郷で88歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Lucombe of Stevenstone(이)가 1405년 영국 켄트 이스트웰에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Lucombe III(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Margaret Cornwall Kayle(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1429년 영국에서 Sir Walter Moyle(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1493년 6월 1일 그녀의 고향에서 88세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Margaret Lucombe of Stevenstone родилась в 1405 г. в местности эаствелл, кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени John Lucombe III было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Margaret Cornwall Kayle, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Walter Moyle в 1429 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 1 июня 1493 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 88 лет.
